concerns.text: T013 instructs downloading data from 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/numenta/NAB/master/data/...' (NAB dataset) instead of the CHERRL repository specified in spec.md (FR-001) and plan.md. This is a missing task for the required CHERRL data ingestion, creating a fatal scope mismatch where the implementation will fetch the wrong dataset.

T032 implements a Wilcoxon signed-rank test, but spec.md FR-005 explicitly mandates a 'paired t-test'. There is no task covering the required paired t-test implementation, and the existing task contradicts the functional requirement, leaving the statistical significance claim (SC-002) unsupported by the plan.

T030 implements the independence check but lacks the explicit 'halt' logic required by spec.md FR-006 and plan.md (Constitution Check VI) if correlation > 0.8. The task description does not cover the mandatory error emission and pipeline stop, leaving the circular validation risk unmitigated in the implementation plan.

T023 generates 'trajectories_divergence.csv' with a 'hacked_label' column. However, spec.md FR-001 (US1) requires the ingestion output to contain *only* G(t) and ΔG(t). The binary label is a product of the detection phase (FR-003/US2). There is no task correctly separating the ingestion output from the detection output, conflating two distinct phases.

T032 references a baseline of 'last [deferred] of training steps'. While the spec defers the value, the task should define the implementation step (e.g., 'implement logic to calculate baseline from last X% of steps where X is a configurable parameter') rather than carrying forward an unresolved placeholder string that cannot be coded.
